evals.prompt: 我想把 uptime-kuma 部署到 sealos，GitHub: https://github.com/louislam/uptime-kuma
evals.expected_output: High readiness score (7+), identifies Node.js project with external services, recommends proceeding with deployment

assertions.name: identifies-nodejs
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Node.js

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 (deployable threshold)

assertions.name: detects-external-db
assertions.description: Detects external database dependency (SQLite/MariaDB)

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report with dimensions and scores

assertions.name: creates-log-file
assertions.description: Creates a deploy log file at ~/.sealos/logs/

evals.expected_output: Eligibility gate identifies a CLI-only target, reports an ineligible decision with evidence, and stops before readiness scoring, Dockerfile generation, build, or template generation without writing project files

assertions.name: identifies-cli-tool
assertions.description: Correctly identifies bat as a CLI tool / non-web application

assertions.name: eligibility-ineligible
assertions.description: Classifies the selected target as ineligible before readiness scoring

assertions.name: recommends-stop
assertions.description: Explicitly recommends NOT deploying / STOP

assertions.name: stops-before-build
assertions.description: Does not generate a Dockerfile, build image, or generate a template

evals.expected_output: Eligibility gate cannot establish a supported deployment unit for the skills/tooling repository and fails closed before readiness scoring or build

assertions.name: identifies-skill-pack
assertions.description: Identifies the current repository as a skills pack or tooling repo rather than a standalone web app

assertions.name: eligibility-unresolved
assertions.description: Returns ineligible or needs_review because no supported deployment unit is proven

assertions.name: recommends-stop
assertions.description: Explicitly recommends NOT deploying / STOP

assertions.name: stops-before-build
assertions.description: Does not generate a Dockerfile, build image, or generate a template

evals.expected_output: Eligibility gate identifies an Electron desktop-only unit, reports DESKTOP_GUI_ONLY evidence, and stops before readiness scoring, Dockerfile generation, build, or template generation without writing project files

assertions.description: Correctly identifies drawio-desktop as an Electron / desktop GUI application

assertions.name: eligibility-ineligible
assertions.description: Returns an ineligible desktop_gui decision with DESKTOP_GUI_ONLY evidence

assertions.name: recommends-stop
assertions.description: Explicitly recommends NOT deploying / STOP

assertions.name: stops-before-build
assertions.description: Does not score readiness, generate a Dockerfile, build image, or generate a template

evals.expected_output: Maximum readiness score (12/12), identifies Rust web application with Rocket framework and existing Dockerfile, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: score-above-4

assertions.name: identifies-web-service

assertions.name: produces-structured-report

evals.expected_output: Eligibility gate identifies Flask itself as a framework/library rather than a deployable application and stops before readiness scoring or build

assertions.name: identifies-library
assertions.description: Correctly identifies Flask as a framework/library, not a standalone deployable app

assertions.name: eligibility-ineligible
assertions.description: Classifies the selected target as a library or otherwise unresolved, not as a web service

assertions.name: explains-not-standalone
assertions.description: Explains why this repository is not directly deployable as a service

assertions.name: stops-before-build
assertions.description: Does not generate a Dockerfile, build image, or generate a template

evals.expected_output: Detects the published IT Tools image without misclassifying ghcr.io as Docker Hub, prefers a verified concrete version tag when package metadata provides one, and generates a template from the reusable image path

assertions.name: detects-ghcr-image
assertions.description: Detects ghcr.io/corentinth/it-tools as a reusable image candidate

assertions.name: does-not-emit-fake-dockerhub-ghcr
assertions.description: Does not report docker.io/ghcr.io/corentinth or otherwise treat ghcr.io as a Docker Hub namespace

assertions.name: prefers-concrete-tag
assertions.description: Uses a verified concrete version tag such as 2024.10.22-7ca5933 when package metadata provides that version, while allowing explicit floating tags only as fallback

assertions.name: uses-reuse-image-path
assertions.description: Writes reuse-image build artifacts and skips unnecessary image build work

evals.prompt: Classify this deployment target before continuing: package.json has scripts.start='node server.js', dependencies.express='^5', and devDependencies.electron='^35'. There are no Electron scripts, desktop API imports, or desktop packaging files.
evals.expected_output: Treats Electron as an uncorroborated dependency signal rather than a desktop entry point, identifies the Express start command as a web_service candidate, and allows deployment eligibility to pass

assertions.name: does-not-reject-on-dependency-alone
assertions.description: Does not return desktop_gui or DESKTOP_GUI_ONLY solely because Electron appears in devDependencies

assertions.name: uses-server-entrypoint-evidence
assertions.description: Uses the Express dependency plus node server start command as affirmative web-service evidence

assertions.name: eligibility-passes
assertions.description: Returns an eligible web_service decision and permits readiness assessment to continue

evals.prompt: Prepare a Mastodon-style app whose analysis classifies SMTP_PORT as an optional input with the default string "587". Expose it as spec.inputs.smtp_port, keep infrastructure replicas and ports numeric, validate the generated template, and stop after the prepare-only delivery artifacts are complete.
evals.expected_output: Writes a prepare-only Sealos Template whose SMTP port default remains the YAML string "587", preserves numeric Kubernetes infrastructure fields, runs the docker-to-sealos consistency checker against .sealos/template/index.yaml so R052 blocks an unquoted default, and finishes with delivery-manifest.json without deploying.

assertions.name: smtp-default-is-string
assertions.description: Emits spec.inputs.smtp_port.default as a quoted YAML string such as "587", never as the numeric scalar 587

assertions.name: input-reference-is-declared
assertions.description: Declares spec.inputs.smtp_port and references that declared input from the Mastodon SMTP_PORT environment value

assertions.name: infrastructure-numbers-stay-numeric
assertions.description: Keeps Kubernetes replicas, container ports, and Service ports as numeric YAML fields

assertions.name: validates-generated-artifact
assertions.description: Runs check_consistency.py with --artifacts pointing to .sealos/template/index.yaml before the pull-secret patcher or delivery manifest

assertions.name: rejects-unquoted-input-default
assertions.description: Treats R052 or the equivalent string-type validation failure as blocking and fixes the template before continuing

assertions.name: remains-prepare-only
assertions.description: Writes .sealos/delivery-manifest.json and does not invoke Template API deployment, UPDATE mode, rollout, rollback, or runtime smoke verification

evals.prompt: /sealos-deploy https://github.com/danny-avila/LibreChat，使用官方多组件 Compose；模板中 RAG 固定使用 OpenAI embeddings，并且 PostgreSQL 需要 vector 扩展。只允许生成该 skill 已有的产物。
evals.expected_output: Generates the existing Sealos template artifact with pinned component images, official health probes, valid runtime credentials, and a database final-state gate; the complete docker-to-sealos quality gate passes before the existing delivery manifest is written, with no new artifact type introduced

assertions.name: pins-component-images
assertions.description: Resolves :latest or floating LibreChat component tags to an explicit version tag or digest

assertions.name: uses-official-rag-health
assertions.description: Uses /health on port 8000 for LibreChat RAG probes instead of probing /

assertions.name: validates-format-constrained-credentials
assertions.description: Does not use bare random() defaults for LibreChat CREDS_KEY/CREDS_IV hex contracts

assertions.name: requires-selected-provider-credential
assertions.description: OpenAI embeddings is paired with a non-empty required OpenAI credential rather than an optional empty input

assertions.name: gates-rag-on-vector-state
assertions.description: RAG has an initContainer gate that waits for PostgreSQL and verifies the vector extension before startup

assertions.name: classifies-mongodb-as-database
assertions.description: Converts the official mongo:8.0.20 service into the pinned MongoDB KubeBlocks Cluster contract

assertions.name: rejects-raw-mongodb-workload
assertions.description: Emits no MongoDB Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, CronJob, or standalone Service

assertions.name: does-not-assume-ghcr-private
assertions.description: Does not add imagePullSecrets solely because an image is hosted on GHCR

assertions.name: fails-closed-before-manifest
assertions.description: Runs the complete docker-to-sealos quality gate after final template mutation and does not write delivery-manifest.json on failure

assertions.name: keeps-existing-artifact-inventory
assertions.description: Introduces no generated artifact beyond the skill's existing analysis, build request/result, template, Dockerfile, and delivery manifest outputs
